title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio · Program Brief: Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval
summary: A maintenance assistant extracts only PDF text, loses diagrams and table headers, cites whole pages, and presents visual evidence without text alternatives. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multimodal accessibility eval increment in accessible multimodal evidence retriever. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality.
Align transcript, speaker, time, scene, slide, caption, confidence, and media segment while handling corrections and access policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

theory case — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6623 during an incident-response knowledge search. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example RAG engineering team handles case 6623 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide structured text alternatives and keyboard inspection and evaluate retrieval, grounding, citations, bias, injection, latency, and user comprehension by modality. A defensible solution applies Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to accessible multimodal evidence retriever.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-multimodal-rag-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-multimodal-rag-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_multimodal_rag_4(pages: int = 8, text_regions: int = 20, image_regions: int = 6, table_regions: int = 4, coordinates_kept: bool = True, text_alternatives: bool = True, modality_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if pages <= 0 or min(text_regions, image_regions, table_regions) < 0:
        return False, 0, "document-regions"
    if not coordinates_kept:
        return False, pages, "visual-citation"
    if not text_alternatives:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-alternative"
    if not modality_evaluated:
        return False, pages, "modality-quality"
    regions = text_regions + image_regions + table_regions
    assert regions >= pages
    return True, regions, "multimodal-evidence"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python RAG decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected query or corpus case, then state which native model, vector-store, provider, network, load, privacy, security, recovery, or deployment claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 3385 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
